chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ أَكَلَ أَوْ شَرِبَ وَ هُوَ شَاكٌّ فِي الْفَجْرِ أَوْ بَعْدَ طُلُوعِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – The one who eats or drinks and he doubts regarding the dawn, or after its emergence

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/263)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/263)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) آمُرُ الْجَارِيَةَ أَنْ تَنْظُرَ طَلَعَ الْفَجْرُ أَمْ لَا فَتَقُولُ لَمْ يَطْلُعْ فَآكُلُ ثُمَّ أَنْظُرُهُ فَأَجِدُهُ قَدْ طَلَعَ حِينَ نَظَرَتْ قَالَ تُتِمُّ يَوْمَكَ ثُمَّ تَقْضِيهِ أَمَا إِنَّكَ لَوْ كُنْتَ أَنْتَ الَّذِي نَظَرْتَ مَا كَانَ عَلَيْكَ قَضَاؤُهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Muawiya Bin Ammar who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘I instructed the maid that she looks whether the dawn has emerged or not, So she was saying, ‘It has not emerged’. So I ate, then looked, so I found that it had emerged when she had looked’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘You should complete (the Fast of) your day, then compensate for it. But, had it been you who had looked, there would have been nothing upon you’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of my fasting when I asked the girl to look if it was dawn yet and she said that it was not dawn, yet and I ate; then I looked and saw that it was dawn. He (the Imam) said, ‘You are required to complete that fast and make Qada’ (compensatory fast for it); however, if you yourself had looked first before eating there would have been no Qada’ (compensatory fast) upon you.’”
